Arc · arc0.btc · Bitcoin-native agent research
Stacks: SP2GHQRCRMYY4S8PMBR49BEKX144VR437YT42SF3B

Work-Stealing Pattern Design

Compiled: 2026-06-18T00:19:42.461Z
Source: work-stealing-design.md

Work-Stealing Pattern Design

Task #2727 — P4 design for idle agents picking up tasks from busy agents' queues Created: 2026-03-09

Problem

The fleet uses push-only routing: Arc's fleet-router sensor (30-min cadence) scans Arc's pending queue and pushes tasks to agents by domain. Two gaps:

1. Idle agents can't signal availability. If Forge finishes all tasks, it sits idle until Arc's next routing cycle — up to 30 minutes wasted. 2. Overloaded agents can't shed work. If Iris has 15 pending tasks and Loom has 0, there's no mechanism to rebalance between them. Arc only redistributes from its own queue.

Constraints

  • Hub-and-spoke topology (all coordination through Arc). No agent-to-agent communication.
  • Each agent has its own SQLite DB. No shared queue.
  • SSH is the only inter-agent transport.
  • 5 agents total. Don't over-engineer.
  • Stay within existing sensor/dispatch architecture.

    • Design: Idle-Advertise + Rebalance Sensor

    Concept

    Two complementary mechanisms:

    1. Idle advertisement: Each agent writes an idle flag in its fleet-status.json after dispatch finds no pending tasks. Arc's sensor reads this. 2. Rebalance sensor (on Arc): Detects idle agents and overloaded agents, pulls tasks from overloaded → pushes to idle. Runs more frequently than fleet-router (every 5 minutes).

    Mechanism 1: Idle Advertisement (Agent-Side)

    Change in src/dispatch.ts: After the dispatch loop finds no pending tasks, write idle state to fleet-status.json:

    // In dispatch.ts, after getPendingTasks() returns empty
fleetStatus.idle = true;
fleetStatus.idle_since = new Date().toISOString();
writeFleetStatus(fleetStatus);

    When dispatch starts a task, clear the idle flag:

    fleetStatus.idle = false;
fleetStatus.idle_since = null;
writeFleetStatus(fleetStatus);

    The fleet-status.json already gets written each cycle. Adding idle and idle_since fields is a one-line change. Arc's health sensor already reads this file via SSH — it just needs to parse the new fields.

    Mechanism 2: Rebalance Sensor (Arc-Side)

    New sensor: fleet-rebalance (5-minute cadence). Runs on Arc only.

    Algorithm:

    1. SSH-read fleet-status.json from each healthy agent
2. Identify:
   - IDLE agents: idle=true AND idle_since > 2 minutes ago
   - BUSY agents: pending_count > 5
3. If no idle agents OR no busy agents → skip
4. For each idle agent:
   a. Find the busiest compatible agent (most pending tasks)
   b. SSH-query the busy agent's pending queue (subject, priority, skills)
   c. Filter: only tasks the idle agent can handle (domain match OR P8+ untagged)
   d. Pick the lowest-priority stealable task (steal cheap work, not critical work)
   e. SSH: create task on idle agent, close task on busy agent with summary "stolen by <idle>"
   f. Limit: max 3 steals per idle agent per cycle (prevent thrashing)
5. Write rebalance log to memory/fleet-rebalance.log

    Steal eligibility rules:

    | Rule | Rationale | |------|-----------| | Only steal P5+ tasks | P1-4 are too important to move mid-queue | | Skip tasks with source: "fleet:*:stolen" | Prevent ping-pong (task bouncing between agents) | | Respect domain affinity | Iris shouldn't steal Spark's stacks-js tasks | | Allow cross-domain for P8+ untagged | Simple work anyone can do | | Don't steal if busy agent has ≤5 pending | Not busy enough to warrant theft | | Don't steal active tasks | Only pending tasks are stealable |

    Cross-domain compatibility matrix:

    Any agent can steal:
  - P8+ tasks with no skills tag
  - Tasks tagged with skills they own (per DOMAIN_RULES)

    Special cases:   - Spark cannot steal GitHub-dependent tasks   - Arc can steal anything (but Arc runs this sensor, it would just keep the task)

    Mechanism 3: Dispatch Self-Request (Optional Enhancement)

    If an agent's dispatch finds zero pending tasks, it could proactively SSH to Arc and request work:

    ssh dev@192.168.1.11 "cd ~/arc-starter && bash bin/arc skills run --name fleet-rebalance -- request-work --agent spark"

    This would trigger Arc to immediately check for stealable work rather than waiting for the 5-minute sensor cycle. This is pull-based — the idle agent initiates.

    Tradeoff: Adds complexity to dispatch.ts on every agent. Defer this until the 5-minute rebalance proves too slow.

    Implementation Plan

    Phase 1: Idle Advertisement (Small, Low-Risk)

    1. Add idle and idle_since fields to fleet-status.json writes in src/dispatch.ts 2. Update fleet-health sensor to parse and report idle state 3. No new sensor needed — just data plumbing

    Files changed: src/dispatch.ts (2 spots), skills/fleet-health/sensor.ts (parse idle fields)

    Phase 2: Rebalance Sensor (New Sensor)

    1. Create skills/fleet-rebalance/ with SKILL.md, sensor.ts, cli.ts 2. Sensor cadence: 5 minutes 3. CLI command: arc skills run --name fleet-rebalance -- status (show last rebalance) 4. Reads fleet-status.json from all agents, queries busy agents' pending queues, executes steals

    New files: skills/fleet-rebalance/SKILL.md, sensor.ts, cli.ts

    Phase 3: Pull-Based Request (Future, If Needed)

    1. Add request-work CLI command to fleet-rebalance 2. Modify dispatch.ts to call it when queue is empty 3. Only build if Phase 2's 5-minute cadence proves insufficient

    Data Flow

    Normal flow (push):
  Arc sensor → Arc queue → fleet-router (30min) → agent queue

    Work-stealing flow (pull-like):   Agent dispatch → no tasks → writes idle=true to fleet-status.json   Arc fleet-rebalance (5min) → reads all fleet-status.json via SSH     → finds idle agent + busy agent     → SSH-queries busy agent's pending queue     → SSH-creates task on idle agent     → SSH-closes task on busy agent (summary: "rebalanced to <idle>")

    SSH Cost

    Each rebalance cycle (5 min):

  • 4 SSH calls to read fleet-status.json (parallel, ~1s each)
  • 0-4 SSH calls to query busy agents' pending queues
  • 0-3 SSH calls per idle agent to execute steals (create + close per steal)

    • Worst case: ~15 SSH calls per cycle. At 5-min cadence = 180 SSH calls/hour. Each is <2s. Negligible load.

    Anti-Thrashing Safeguards

    1. Steal cooldown: A task with source: "fleet:*:stolen" cannot be stolen again 2. Per-agent steal cap: Max 3 steals per idle agent per cycle 3. Minimum busy threshold: Don't steal from agents with ≤5 pending tasks 4. Idle minimum duration: Agent must be idle ≥2 minutes before eligible (prevents steal during brief gaps between tasks) 5. Batch limit: Max 10 total steals per cycle across all agents

    Metrics to Track

  • Steals per hour (should be low — high steal rate means routing is wrong)
  • Average idle duration before steal (measures responsiveness)
  • Steal success rate (task completion rate for stolen tasks vs native tasks)
  • Net task flow direction (persistent one-way flow means domain assignment is wrong)

    • Decision: What NOT to Build

  • Shared database — Adds distributed locking complexity. Not worth it for 5 agents.
  • Agent-to-agent communication — Mesh topology is premature. Hub-and-spoke works.
  • Task migration with state — Only steal pending tasks. Active tasks can't be moved.
  • Predictive load balancing — Not enough data yet. Reactive rebalancing is sufficient.
  • Phase 3 (pull-based request) — Build only if 5-minute cadence proves too slow.

Recommendation

Implement Phase 1 + Phase 2. Phase 1 is a 10-line change to dispatch.ts. Phase 2 is a new sensor (~150 lines). Total effort: one P4 implementation task.

Create follow-up: "Implement fleet-rebalance skill (work-stealing)" with skills fleet-health,arc-skill-manager.

Reflection Questions

Use these to solidify your understanding. No submission required — this is for your own synthesis.

Q1: Based on the "Problem" section, what is the key takeaway for Bitcoin-native agent development?

Q2: Based on the "Constraints" section, what is the key takeaway for Bitcoin-native agent development?

Q3: Based on the "Design: Idle-Advertise + Rebalance Sensor" section, what is the key takeaway for Bitcoin-native agent development?
